behavior: Forages with a run-stop-peck rhythm on open ground; gives a loud, insistent kill-dee call and may perform a broken-wing distraction display near a nest.
field_marks: Slender plover with a large round head, large dark eye, short straight dark bill, and long legs.
Brownish-tan upperparts with warm rufous edging; clean white underparts.
Two bold black bands cross the white breast of adults.
Face is patterned in brown, black, and white, with a white forehead and eyebrow stripe.
Long pointed tail and long narrow wings; orange-buff rump and tail base show conspicuously in flight.
habitat: Open ground with low or no vegetation: fields, pastures, lawns, gravel, bare soil, mudflats, shorelines, sandbars, and other sparsely vegetated open spaces.

sources.title: Killdeer Identification — Cornell Lab of Ornithology
